name: Mustardy cauliflower cheese
intro: of lime. This can be made up to the point of baking and stored in the fridge for 1

ingredients.item: large cauliflower, broken into roughly 1½ inch/4cm  orets (7 cups/700g)
green chiles, seeded and  nely diced
oz/120g aged Cheddar, coarsely grated salt
cup/15g fresh white breadcrumbs (from about ½ slice, crust left on if soft)

method: Put the butter into a 9-inch/24cm round casserole pan or ovenproof dish of a similar size and place over medium heat. Add the onion and sauté for 8 minutes, until soft and golden. Add the cumin, curry powder, mustard powder, and chiles and cook for 4 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the mustard seeds, cook for 1 minute, then pour in the cream. Add 1¼ cups/100g of Cheddar and ½ tsp of salt and simmer for 2–3 minutes, until the sauce slightly thickens. Add the cauliower, stir gently, and simmer for 1 minute before removing from the heat.

Place the remaining ¼ cup/20g of Cheddar in a bowl and add the breadcrumbs and parsley. Mix, then sprinkle over the cauliower. Wipe the top inside edge of the pan clean (with a spatula or cloth)—any cream there will burn—and place in the oven. Bake for 8 minutes, until the sauce is bubbling and the cauliower is hot. Turn the broiler to high and keep the pan underneath for 4 minutes, or until the top is golden and crisp. Keep an eye on it so that it does not burn. Remove from the oven and allow to cool a little—just for 5 minutes or so—before serving.

Keep all the leaves on the head of cauliower—they are deliciously crisp and tasty when roasted. I like to serve this in the center of the table, for people to share with drinks at the start of a meal. We break the cauliower apart with our hands, dipping the individual orets and crispy green leaves into the green tahini sauce and sprinkling with salt. If that sounds a bit odd or messy (which it’s actually not, surprisingly), you can always cut the cauliower into four wedges and serve on individual plates, to be eaten more traditionally with a knife and fork! Either way, the tahini sauce is entirely optional. I love it, but just a squeeze of lemon or a dollop of crème fraîche also work very well.
